content.full_text:
May 12, 1976 
Mr. Bruce Adams 
Coopers and Lybrand 
One Bush Street 
San Francisco, Californi~ 
Dear Mr. Adams: 
This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of May 10, 
1976, concerning campaig~ disclosure requirements of the Political 
Reform Act. 
You stated the following facts: 
(1) The California Labor 
Federation plans to sendHto its members, at a cost which will 
exceed $500, a letter st_ting its position on Proposition 15; 
(2) there has been no coqperation, mutual understanding or agree-
ment between the California Labor Federation and its political 
arm, COPE, with regard tq the mailing; (J) although the Federation 
will pay for the mailing ,from membership dues, no portion of the 
dues was earmarked for pqlitical purposes when collected; and 
(4) the Federation has n,ver used dues for political purposes. 
I informed you that unde~ these circumstances, the Federation will 
become a committee under~overnment Code Section 8201J(b) and 
should file Form 460 to ~eport the costs directly attributable to 
the communication. 
Enclqsed is a copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Section 18225 which discqsses the items which should be included 
in computing the cost of ~he mailing. 
,.. 
If you have further quest~ons, please contact our staff. 
NH:mak 
Enclosure 
bee: 
Jeanne 
Sincerely, 
NAN HAMBLETON 
Special Compliance Representative

sections.conclusion: I informed you that under these circumstances, the Federation will become a committee under Government Code Section 82013(b) and should file Form 460 to report the costs directly attributable to the communication.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the California Labor Federation's planned mailing to members regarding Proposition 15, costing over $500, trigger campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act, and what reporting obligations arise?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The California Labor Federation must register as a committee under Government Code Section 82013(b) and file Form 460 to report the costs directly attributable to the Proposition 15 communication mailing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains substantive Q&A in narrative format. Facts are clearly stated and advice is explicitly provided. Minor document quality issues (some characters appear corrupted or unclear) but core content is extractable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the California Labor Federation's planned mailing to members regarding Proposition 15, costing over $500, trigger campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act, and what reporting obligations arise?

CONCLUSION: I informed you that under these circumstances, the Federation will become a committee under Government Code Section 82013(b) and should file Form 460 to report the costs directly attributable to the communication.

Enclqsed is a copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225 which discqsses the items which should be included in computing the cost of ~he mailing. ,.. embedding.summary: FPPC advises that the California Labor Federation's planned mailing to members regarding Proposition 15, exceeding $500 in cost, triggers campaign committee registration and Form 460 reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC provides guidance on what costs should be included in the disclosure calculation.
